reasoning: The document contains a critical defect where header and footer elements are pervasively retained across 24 pages, resulting in visual duplication that impacts screen reader users on nearly every page. Additionally, there are 6 major defects, including 51 instances of truncated image alt text, split tables, bare URLs, and numbered items misclassified as headings. Per the count-based routing thresholds, any critical defect or more than 5 major defects mandates a REJECT outcome.

concerns.description: Header/footer elements retained in output (52): NEUSE BASIN MODELING - DATA COLLECTION DRAFT REPORT; April 2009; - ii -
concerns.analysis: header_footer_count=52, pages_affected=24/24. Header/footer text from extraction JSON is present in the rendered HTML across most pages — pervasive pipeline failure. Screen readers will encounter duplicate artifacts on every page.

In the 'Referenced Figures' section at the end of the HTML output, the text 'MOFFATT & NICHOL' from the document's footer is extracted and rendered multiple times sequentially. This results in visual duplication of the same paragraph text block twice in a row, repeatedly.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Footer containing 'MOFFATT & NICHOL' appearing once per page.
concerns.location: End of document, under 'Referenced Figures' heading

concerns.description: Table split across page break(s): 1 table(s) with repeated headers across consecutive pages
concerns.analysis: split_table_count=1. Examples: pages 18-19: 14 cols (permit, owner, facility...). Tables spanning multiple pages were extracted as separate table elements with duplicated header rows. The HTML output will show multiple tables instead of one continuous table — a structural accessibility issue.

concerns.description: Output has more images than source PDF (ratio 1.1x) — possible image duplication
concerns.analysis: image_count_ratio=1.06. More images in output than in source indicates duplicated or fabricated image elements.

concerns.description: 5 bare URL(s) in text not converted to hyperlinks: http://www.agr.state.nc.us/stats/release/WU2007; http://www.ncwater.org/whichsystem.php; <http://lwf.ncdc.noaa.gov/oa/ncdc.html>; <http://www.agcensus.usda.gov/Publications/2007/index.asp>; http://www.census.gov/prod/www/abs/decennial/index.htm>
concerns.analysis: bare_url_count=5. URLs appear as plain text instead of clickable links. Per NCDIT criteria, link text not wrapped in href is a major defect.

concerns.description: Numbered items as headings: 4 heading(s) look like numbered list items
concerns.analysis: heading_list_count=4. Headings starting with '1.', '2.' etc. suggest numbered list items were misclassified as headings during extraction.

concerns.description: Incomplete image alt text: 51 image(s) with inadequate descriptions (51 truncated mid-sentence) — e.g. p1: "...iangles that display white silhouettes of a flying"; p1: "... stylized, geometric blue and gray letters 'M' and"; p2: "...tions with white silhouettes of a bird over water,"
concerns.analysis: incomplete_alt_text=51 (truncated=51, placeholder=0, empty=0). Per WCAG 2.1 AA, meaningful images must have descriptive alternative text for screen-reader accessibility.

concerns.description: Displaced hyperlinks: 2 link(s) extracted as standalone elements instead of inline text
concerns.analysis: standalone_links=2, examples=['http://www.agr.state.nc.us/plantindustry/NurseryBook/search.', 'http://nc.water.usgs.gov/infodata/wateruse/data/Data_Tables_']. Links should be inline within paragraphs, not separate blocks.

concerns.description: Table header markup issues: 1 table(s) with 3+ rows have no <th> header cells
concerns.analysis: data_in_th_time=0, no_th_tables=1. Minor semantic markup issues: tables lack <th> elements or use time values as headers. These affect screen reader context but are less severe than data misrepresentation.

concerns.description: Source PDF contains 3 instance(s) of blue-styled text without hyperlink annotations. These appear to be intended as hyperlinks but were not annotated in the PDF (likely lost during Word-to-PDF export). This is a source document limitation, not a pipeline defect. Examples: page 11: "http://www.agr.state.nc.us/plantindustry/"; page 11: "NurseryBook/search.asp"; page 11: "http://nc.water.usgs.gov/infodata/wateruse/data/Da"
concerns.analysis: orphaned_blue_text_count=3. Blue text without link annotations detected via PyMuPDF color extraction.
